title: Fail loudly when CI guard cannot verify, not vacuously pass
context: v0.12.0's constraint guard was passing all PRs silently when preconditions failed: missing base branch ref (actions/checkout creates no tracking ref), unresolvable --base flags, or guard process crashes. Empty diffs were indistinguishable from 'no violations found.' Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file).
decision: Invert the failure mode: require explicit precondition checks (ref must exist), explicit fetch steps, explicit exit code defaults (1 on missing output), and explicit error handling. Policy: inability to verify compliance → fail loudly with a clear error message, not a silent pass.
consequences: PRs with missing base branch fetch now fail (guard breaks early, not late)
Developers see explicit errors ('--base ref X does not resolve') instead of silent passes
Guard process crashes default to exit 1, preventing vacuous passes from unrecoverable failures
CI becomes a reliable enforcement mechanism, not a rubber stamp
Error recovery is clearer: developers know exactly what to fix
alternatives_rejected: Continue silently passing when base ref is missing or unresolvable (the bug)
Treat empty diff as 'no changes = compliant' (conflates missing data with verification)
Fail the CI job with a silent exit 0 on auth/ref errors (prevents detection)
